data_in.description: Publicly available Hi-C FASTQ accession (e.g., NCBI SRA, GEO, or ENCODE-DCC repository accession)

data_out.description: Hi-C contact map in ENCODE reference format (.hic file) with verified checksum matching reference

domain_knowledge: The ENCODE Hi-C uniform processing pipeline is a standardized wrapper around Juicer that ensures consistent kilobase-resolution Hi-C map generation from raw FASTQ files across ENCODE submissions.
Hi-C contact maps are typically stored in binary .hic format, which encodes chromosome pairs, interaction counts, and resolution-specific normalization matrices for efficient random access.
Checksum verification (e.g., MD5 or SHA256) is the standard reproducibility assurance for ENCODE pipeline outputs, confirming deterministic pipeline execution on identical inputs.
The pipeline accepts paired-end Hi-C FASTQ files and produces alignment, duplicate filtering, and contact binning steps before generating the final .hic file.

direct_checks: verify that ENCODE-DCC/hic-pipeline repository is accessible and contains executable pipeline code
verify that a public Hi-C FASTQ accession (SRA/GEO format) can be retrieved and loaded
verify that pipeline execution completes without fatal errors on the selected accession
verify that output Hi-C map file exists in expected format (e.g., .hic or standard matrix format)
verify that output checksum or file hash can be computed and compared against documented ENCODE reference checksum (if reference checksum is available in repository or documentation)
expert_review: assess whether the computed Hi-C map reflects expected contact frequency patterns and chromosome structure characteristic of valid Hi-C data
review whether pipeline parameter settings align with ENCODE uniform processing standards
evaluate whether any deviations from reference output (if detected) are attributable to software versions, reference genome builds, or acceptable preprocessing variations rather than pipeline failure

Clone the ENCODE Hi-C uniform processing pipeline from ENCODE-DCC/hic-pipeline.

task_description: Execute the ENCODE Hi-C uniform processing pipeline (encode_hic_pipeline) on a publicly available Hi-C FASTQ accession to generate a kilobase-resolution Hi-C map, and verify that the output matches ENCODE reference format and checksum.

task_inputs.label: Hi-C FASTQ dataset accession or file path

task_objective: Execute the ENCODE Hi-C pipeline on a public Hi-C FASTQ accession and validate output format and checksum against reference standards.
title: Reproduce the ENCODE Hi-C Uniform Processing Pipeline Output Using the Juicer-Based ENCODE Workflow
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Obtain a publicly available Hi-C FASTQ dataset from a public repository (e.g., NCBI SRA, GEO) or use an ENCODE-deposited accession. 2. Clone the ENCODE Hi-C uniform processing pipeline from the ENCODE-DCC/hic-pipeline repository. 3. Run the encode_hic_pipeline wrapper on the FASTQ input files to generate aligned reads and construct the Hi-C contact map. 4. Validate the output Hi-C map file format (e.g., .hic binary format) and compute checksum or file hash. 5. Compare the computed checksum against the ENCODE reference output checksum to confirm pipeline reproducibility and correctness.
